description: Wave 8 — Production Launch Hardening. Closes every loop-buildable gap between the Wave 7 feature-complete state and a public production release, under the locked product shape: the PUBLIC surface is a spectator website (lobby, live watch, ladder, recap) served exclusively from /public/* endpoints; everything else (admin, ingest, gauntlets, leagues, scheduler, metrics) runs on a PRIVATE backend that is never exposed to the internet. Build order: P0 surface split (110-111) -> P1 trust + ops rails (112-116) -> P2 verification + flake burn-down (117-118) -> P3 deploy + perf (119-120). Human-gated launch steps (key rotation, domain/TLS/CDN/Postgres provisioning, cap/cadence sign-off, legal pages, one paid integration run) are NOT stories — see the launch checklist in the session plan; the loop must reach loop-complete without any new credential.

userStories.title: Public-surface-only API mode
userStories.description: As the operator, I need the API to run in a mode that serves ONLY the public spectator surface, so the internet-facing process physically cannot expose admin, ingest, or any private route — the architectural embodiment of 'website public, everything else private'.
acceptanceCriteria: New setting `padrino_public_surface_only: bool = False`; when True, `create_app` mounts ONLY the public router and the health router — admin, admin_keys, ingest, games, leagues, gauntlets, and scheduled_gauntlets routers are not mounted at all (404, not 401/403).
`/metrics` is NOT served in public-surface-only mode (scraping happens against the internal instance).
`/healthz` and `/readyz` still work in public mode (the edge needs probes).

Test `tests/api/test_public_surface_only.py` asserts: with the flag on, every private route prefix 404s, `/public/live`, `/public/recent`, `/public/ladder`, `/public/games/{id}/live` and `/healthz` work; with the flag off (default) behavior is unchanged.
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run ruff format --check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes
userStories.notes: P0. Defense in depth: even a reverse-proxy misconfiguration cannot leak a private route, because the public process never registers them.

userStories.title: Spectator website consumes only /public endpoints
userStories.description: As a public visitor, every page I can reach must work against the public-surface-only API, so the deployed website functions with zero access to private routes.
acceptanceCriteria: Audit `web/dashboard/src/routes/**`: the home page currently calls `listGames` and `listGauntlets` (private `/games`, `/gauntlets`) for KPIs — replace home KPIs with public-surface data (e.g. live-now count from `/public/live`, recent count from `/public/recent` total_estimate, top-3 from `/public/ladder`); no public-reachable page may call a non-/public endpoint.
Private-only pages (e.g. internal games browser) are either removed from the public nav or clearly degrade when the private API is absent — no infinite spinners, no hard errors on the public site.
Playwright `web/dashboard/tests/e2e/home.spec.ts` updated to the new KPI set and passing against the smoke harness.
pnpm -C web/dashboard lint passes
pnpm -C web/dashboard check passes
pnpm -C web/dashboard test:e2e passes (Playwright)
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run ruff format --check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes
userStories.notes: Root cause of the pre-existing home.spec e2e failure: the home page depends on auth-gated private endpoints.

userStories.title: Public surfaces exclude unverified ingested games
userStories.description: As the platform, the public ladder/leaderboards must never include results from self-signed ingested bundles that were not centrally verified, so a submitter-scoped key can never pollute the public rankings.
acceptanceCriteria: Every public rating/analytics surface (public leaderboard, model rollup, ladder, recent index) excludes games originating from `IngestedGame` rows whose `verification_status` is not 'verified'; house-run (non-ingested) games are unaffected.

Test `tests/api/test_public_excludes_unverified.py` seeds one house game, one verified ingest, one unverified ingest, and asserts the unverified one is absent from each public surface while remaining visible on the private/admin surface.
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run ruff format --check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes
userStories.notes: Cheap insurance: ingest is private in this deployment, but the filter makes the trust property hold by construction.

userStories.title: Operational alerting: webhook notifier + alert rules
userStories.description: As the operator of an unattended money-spending system, I need to be notified when something important happens — spend cap reached, scheduler dead, moderation gate degraded — instead of discovering it on the provider bill.
acceptanceCriteria: New `src/padrino/observability/alerts.py` with `AlertNotifier` (async, posts JSON to `padrino_alert_webhook_url: str | None = None`; no-op transport when unset; always emits a structured log).

Alerts fired from existing seams: `spend.cap.reached` (spend governor denial), `scheduler.heartbeat.stale` (checked in the tick against the heartbeats table), `moderation.guard.unavailable` (guard None or erroring while continuous matchmaking enabled), `admission.denied.streak` (N consecutive denials, configurable).
A Prometheus alert-rules file (`docs/deployment/alert-rules.yml`) covering the same conditions from the existing metrics, shipped with the deploy config.

Tests `tests/observability/test_alerts.py` use a stub transport (httpx MockTransport) — no network, no integration marker: each trigger fires exactly once per condition transition (no alert spam every tick).
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run ruff format --check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes
userStories.notes: Webhook URL is set by the human at deploy time (Slack/Discord/etc.); the loop builds and tests the machinery.

userStories.title: Scheduler claim hardening (SKIP LOCKED)
userStories.description: As the operator, two scheduler replicas must never double-claim the same gauntlet, so accidental horizontal scaling (or a stuck-then-restarted instance) cannot corrupt runs.
acceptanceCriteria: `claim_oldest_pending` (runner/scheduler claim path) uses `SELECT ... FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED` on PostgreSQL (`with_for_update(skip_locked=True)`); SQLite path unchanged (single-writer documented in the function docstring).
Postgres-marked test (`@pytest.mark.postgres`) asserts two concurrent claimers receive distinct rows (or one row + one None) — never the same row.
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run ruff format --check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes
userStories.notes: Matters exactly now that continuous matchmaking gives the scheduler a permanent workload.

userStories.title: Honor PADRINO_LOG_LEVEL
userStories.description: As the operator debugging production, the configured log level must actually take effect — cli.py currently hardcodes 'INFO'.
acceptanceCriteria: `padrino serve`, `padrino scheduler`, and `padrino bootstrap` configure logging from `settings.padrino_log_level` (env `PADRINO_LOG_LEVEL`), not a hardcoded literal.
Unit test asserts the logging configuration call receives the settings value (e.g. DEBUG) for at least the serve and scheduler entrypoints.
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run ruff format --check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes

userStories.title: Retention executor job (guarded, dry-run default)
userStories.description: As the operator, the retention planner from US-108 needs an actual executor wired into the scheduler so the compounding game bank stays bounded without manual intervention.
acceptanceCriteria: Scheduler tick gains a retention step gated by `padrino_enable_retention: bool = False` and `padrino_retention_dry_run: bool = True` (both must be explicitly flipped for destructive action).

Executor applies `plan_retention` output: deletes expired non-broadcastable games (cascade), scrubs heavy `llm_calls` payloads past TTL; emits structured logs with counts; dry-run logs the plan without touching rows.

Tests `tests/db/test_retention_executor.py`: dry-run mutates nothing; enabled run deletes/scrubs exactly the planned candidates and NEVER touches broadcastable games, ratings, or public replay data; idempotent on re-run.
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run ruff format --check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes
userStories.notes: US-108 built the pure planner; this is the impure executor it was designed for.

userStories.title: Broadcast pipeline end-to-end smoke test
userStories.description: As the platform, one default-suite test must exercise the ENTIRE Wave 7 spine — matchmaking tick through SSE consumption — so a cross-boundary contract break (like the Wave 7 moderation event-type bug) can never again pass a green suite.
acceptanceCriteria: New `tests/public/test_broadcast_pipeline_e2e.py` (mock LLM adapter, stub guard, zero cadence): run_continuous_matchmaking_tick → game COMPLETED → analytics aggregates written → moderation pass marks broadcastable → LIVE → consume the full SSE stream → every frame validates against the public_event_v1 golden contract → terminal frame is last → mark_recent → recap analytics served spoiler-free.
The test asserts at least one PublicMessageSubmitted frame's text was actually seen by the moderation gate (e.g. via a recording stub guard) — regression-guards the vacuous-gate class of bug.
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run ruff format --check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes
userStories.notes: Lesson from the Wave 7 review: per-story unit tests validated each side of an invented contract; only an end-to-end path catches lockstep-wrong seams.

userStories.title: Flake burn-down: migrations, scheduler timing, dashboard e2e
userStories.description: As the operator, the gates must be trustworthy: every known-flaky or environment-dependent test either becomes hermetic or is properly isolated, so a red CI means a real problem.
acceptanceCriteria: `tests/db/test_migrations_all_dialects.py::test_postgres_full_cycle_upgrade_downgrade_upgrade` passes reliably in the full suite (isolate shared Postgres state per test, e.g. unique schema/database per run).
`tests/runner/test_scheduler.py::test_pending_gauntlet_runs_and_finalizes_to_completed` no longer depends on wall-clock timing under load (injected clock or generous deterministic synchronization).
Dashboard e2e `games.spec.ts` and `leaderboard.spec.ts` are hermetic against the smoke harness (deterministic seeded data, no dependence on machine state); `home.spec.ts` covered by US-111.
Full suite passes 3 consecutive runs locally (`uv run pytest -m "not integration"` ×3) — document the runs in progress.txt.
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run ruff format --check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes
userStories.notes: Known offenders enumerated from the Wave 7 verification runs.

userStories.title: Deploy topology: public edge vs private backend
userStories.description: As the operator, the compose/deploy config must encode the public/private split: a public-api (surface-only) + static dashboard exposed to the edge, and api/scheduler/Postgres reachable only on an internal network.
acceptanceCriteria: `docker-compose.yml` gains a `public-api` service running with `PADRINO_PUBLIC_SURFACE_ONLY=true` and a published port; the full `api` service and `postgres` lose published ports (internal network only); `scheduler` internal; `dashboard` built against the public-api URL.
Compose networks: `edge` (public-api, dashboard) and `internal` (postgres, api, scheduler, public-api) — postgres/api/scheduler are NOT on `edge`.

`docs/deployment/v2-public.md` runbook updated for the split topology, including the reverse-proxy/CDN expectations (TLS termination, caching immutable RECENT responses) and the env vars for spend cap, guard model, alert webhook — this story MAY edit that md as explicit deploy doc.
`tests/docker/test_compose_v2.py` extended (docker-marked, opt-in): compose graph parses, public-api has the flag and a published port, postgres/api/scheduler publish no ports.
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run ruff format --check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes
userStories.notes: Human provisions the actual edge (domain/TLS/CDN); the loop ships config that makes the safe topology the default.

userStories.title: Materialize per-game analytics at RECENT promotion
userStories.description: As the platform serving a public recap page, per-game analytics for finished games should be computed once when the game becomes RECENT instead of re-deriving the full event log on every request.
acceptanceCriteria: On `mark_recent` (or completion of broadcast), the deterministic per-game analytics + claim analysis are computed once and persisted (new table or JSON column keyed by game_id).

`GET /public/games/{id}/analytics` serves the stored result for RECENT games (immutable Cache-Control already in place); LIVE games keep the existing on-the-fly spoiler-safe path; behavior identical from the client's perspective (existing tests keep passing unchanged).
Backfill path: a stored-missing RECENT game falls back to on-the-fly compute and persists the result (self-healing for games promoted before this story).
Test `tests/api/test_game_analytics_materialized.py` asserts the stored row is created at promotion, served on GET, and the fallback persists.
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run ruff format --check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes
userStories.notes: Review finding #14 (deliberately deferred from the --fix pass as architectural).
